must_detect.matched_text:
Incident Report — Cybersecurity and UPSI Handling, Navodya Ventures Limited (IND-X listing), India
On 04-06-2026, our “Saffron Kite” email gateway flagged a phishing reply-chain that exfiltrated draft Q4 results and HR records via https://alerts.navodya-ventures.in/case/INV-24-07?dp=NVL-E%2F0192&upsi=draft-R-30
As a Data Fiduciary under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, we notify that Mr. Raghav Iyer (Employee ID NVL-E/0192; P A N: Q W E P I 1 2 3 4 Z; A a d h a a r token: 7O3O-1I2L-XX9Z) is directly affected.
Files contained his biometric attendance template (fingerprint minutiae hash) and health details (Type 1 diabetes regimen), and note his practicing Jain faith and active membership in the Navashakti Employees Union.
No data about his sex life is stored in payroll systems, and the phrase sexual orientation appears only in a policy template here as benign metadata.
For minor data, a dependant profile for Master Kavish Iyer (age 16; school ID KAV-16-ι; guardian consent checkbox not logged) was attached to the same HR ticket.
Vendor onboarding for Ms. Asha Dev (sole proprietor, GSTIN 99ABCDE1234Z1Z; email asha.dev@devcraft.trade) was in the export, but her bank account numbers and Aadhaar were not collected.
The export also inadvertently attached unpublished price-sensitive information: the unannounced dividend range and a draft acquisition term sheet; trading window remains closed per SRAI PIT norms for IND-X listed entities.
We consider this not a material adverse change to ESG metrics, and no MAC/MAE clause is triggered under the mock share purchase conditions; no MAC addresses were logged by our NAC.
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation
